metadata.description: Name of resources to be deployed.

Deploy Azure Databricks as a second analytics option next to Azure Data Explorer. When enabled, an Azure Databricks workspace is created and automatically configured with the OPC UA Delta tables, Event Hubs ingestion and a sample dashboard.
Provision a Microsoft Fabric F2 capacity as part of the main deployment. Enable this if you plan to deploy Microsoft Fabric after the main deployment.
Object ID of the custom-locations Microsoft Entra application. Obtain it by running az ad sp show --id bc313c14-388c-4e7d-a58e-70017303ee3b --query id -o tsv from the Azure CLI.

protectedSettings.commandToExecute: [concat('bash Bootstrap.sh ''', listkeys(variables('authRuleResourceId'), '2015-08-01').primaryConnectionString, ''' ''', resourceGroup().name, ''' ''', resourceGroup().location, ''' ''', parameters('resourcesName'), ''' ''', reference(concat(parameters('resourcesName'), '-Identity')).clientId, ''' ''', subscription().subscriptionId, ''' ''', parameters('customLocationsOid'), ''' ''', concat(variables('eventHubsNamespaceName'), '.servicebus.windows.net'), '''')]

resources.name: [format('{0}/{1}/{2}', variables('adxClusterName'), 'ontologies','EventHubDataConnection')]
dependsOn: [resourceId('Microsoft.Kusto/clusters/databases/principalAssignments', variables('adxClusterName'), 'ontologies', guid(resourceId('Microsoft.Kusto/clusters/databases', split(format('{0}/{1}', variables('adxClusterName'), 'ontologies'), '/')[0], split(format('{0}/{1}', variables('adxClusterName'), 'ontologies'), '/')[1]), resourceId('Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities', concat(parameters('resourcesName'), '-Identity')), 'Admin'))]

properties.scriptContent:
.create-or-alter function with (folder = "Packages\\Plotly", docstring = "Render interactive graph visualizations using plotly template") plotly_graph_fl ( edges:(*), nodes:(*), node_id_column:string="nodeId", source_id_column:string="sourceId", target_id_column:string="targetId", colorscale_id:string="Cividis", diagram_title:string="Simple Graph for educational purposes") { let pythonCodeBlueprint = ``` import numpy as np import pandas as pd import plotly.graph_objects as go import networkx as nx NODE_OR_EDGE_COLUMN = kargs.get("node_or_edge_column", "tableName") NODE_ROW = kargs.get("node_row", "V") NODE_ID_COLUMN = kargs.get("node_id_column", "nodeId") EDGE_ROW = kargs.get("edge_row", "E") SOURCE_ID_COLUMN = kargs.get("source_id_column", "sourceId") TARGET_ID_COLUMN = kargs.get("target_id_column", "targetId") COLORSCALE_ID = kargs.get("colorscale_id", "YlGnBu") DIAGRAM_TITLE = kargs.get("diagram_title", "Network Graph Visualization") def is_valid_value(value): if value is None or (isinstance(value, str) and value == ''): return False if hasattr(value, '__len__') and not isinstance(value, str): try: na_result = pd.isna(value) return not (hasattr(na_result, 'all') and na_result.all()) and not na_result except (TypeError, ValueError): return True return not pd.isna(value) def create_properties_dict(row): return {k: v for k, v in row.items() if is_valid_value(v)} def build_graph(dataframe): graph = nx.Graph() nodes_df = dataframe[dataframe[NODE_OR_EDGE_COLUMN] == NODE_ROW] edges_df = dataframe[dataframe[NODE_OR_EDGE_COLUMN] == EDGE_ROW] if not nodes_df.empty: for _, row in nodes_df.iterrows(): graph.add_node(row[NODE_ID_COLUMN], size=1, properties=create_properties_dict(row)) if not edges_df.empty: for _, row in edges_df.iterrows(): graph.add_edge(row[SOURCE_ID_COLUMN], row[TARGET_ID_COLUMN], weight=1, properties=create_properties_dict(row)) return graph def create_edge_traces(graph, positions): edge_x, edge_y = [], [] mnode_x, mnode_y, mnode_txt = [], [], [] for source, target, edge_data in graph.edges(data=True): x0, y0 = positions[source] x1, y1 = positions[target] edge_x.extend([x0, x1, None]) edge_y.extend([y0, y1, None]) properties = edge_data.get('properties', {}) valid_props = {k: v for k, v in properties.items() if is_valid_value(v)} prop_text = '
'.join(f'{key}: {value}' for key, value in valid_props.items()) text = f'source: {source} destination: {target}
Properties: {prop_text}' mnode_x.append((x0 + x1) * 0.5) mnode_y.append((y0 + y1) * 0.5) mnode_txt.append(text) edge_trace = go.Scatter(x=edge_x, y=edge_y, line=dict(width=0.5, color='#888'), hoverinfo='none', mode='lines') mnode_trace = go.Scatter(x=mnode_x, y=mnode_y, text=mnode_txt, mode="markers", hoverinfo='text', opacity=0.5, marker=dict(color='LightSkyBlue', size=2, line_width=1)) return edge_trace, mnode_trace def create_node_trace(graph, positions): nodes_list = list(graph.nodes()) num_nodes = len(nodes_list) inv_num_nodes = 1.0 / num_nodes if num_nodes > 0 else 0 node_x = [positions[node][0] for node in nodes_list] node_y = [positions[node][1] for node in nodes_list] node_adjacencies = [] node_text = [] node_sizes = [] for node in nodes_list: num_connections = len(graph[node]) node_adjacencies.append(num_connections) node_sizes.append(10 + num_connections * inv_num_nodes) node_properties = graph.nodes[node].get('properties', {}) valid_props = {k: v for k, v in node_properties.items() if is_valid_value(v)} prop_text = '
'.join(f'{key}: {value}' for key, value in valid_props.items()) text = f'# of connections: {num_connections}
Properties: {prop_text}' node_text.append(text) return go.Scatter(x=node_x, y=node_y, text=node_text, mode='markers', hoverinfo='text', marker=dict(colorscale=COLORSCALE_ID, color=node_adjacencies, size=node_sizes, line=dict(width=2, color='#888'))) def hierarchical_layout(graph): # Top-down hierarchy with EQUAL spacing so ISA-95 levels form evenly spaced rows # and nodes within each row spread evenly. Replaces the uneven spring layout. positions = {} if graph.number_of_nodes() == 0: return positions import networkx as _nx level = {} for component in _nx.connected_components(graph): sub = graph.subgraph(component) root = max(sub.nodes(), key=lambda n: sub.degree(n)) for node, depth in _nx.single_source_shortest_path_length(sub, root).items(): level[node] = depth max_level = max(level.values()) if level else 0 nodes_by_level = {} for node, lvl in level.items(): nodes_by_level.setdefault(lvl, []).append(node) for lvl, nodes_in_level in nodes_by_level.items(): nodes_in_level = sorted(nodes_in_level, key=lambda n: str(n)) count = len(nodes_in_level) for i, node in enumerate(nodes_in_level): x = 0.5 if count == 1 else i / (count - 1) y = 1.0 if max_level == 0 else 1.0 - (lvl / max_level) positions[node] = (x, y) return positions def create_plotly_figure(graph): positions = hierarchical_layout(graph) edge_trace, mnode_trace = create_edge_traces(graph, positions) node_trace = create_node_trace(graph, positions) return go.Figure(data=[edge_trace, node_trace, mnode_trace], layout=go.Layout(title=f'
{DIAGRAM_TITLE}
', showlegend=False, hovermode='closest', margin=dict(b=20, l=5, r=5, t=40), annotations=[dict(text="Created using plotly, networkx and the python plugin of Kusto", showarrow=False, xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0.005, y=-0.002)], xaxis=dict(showgrid=False, zeroline=False, showticklabels=False), yaxis=dict(showgrid=False, zeroline=False, showticklabels=False))) graph = build_graph(df) figure = create_plotly_figure(graph) plotly_obj = figure.to_json() result = pd.DataFrame(data=[plotly_obj], columns=['plotly']) ```; let E = view() { edges }; let V = view() { nodes }; union withsource=tableName E, V | evaluate python( typeof(plotly:string), pythonCodeBlueprint, bag_pack( 'node_or_edge_column', 'tableName', 'node_row', 'V', 'node_id_column', node_id_column, 'edge_row', 'E', 'source_id_column', source_id_column, 'target_id_column', target_id_column, 'colorscale_id', colorscale_id, 'diagram_title', diagram_title )) }

resources.comment: Waits for the ADX cluster to reach a terminal provisioning state before the Event Hub data connections are created. ARM dependsOn only waits for each child resource's own call to return, not for the cluster itself to settle, which intermittently caused 'RequestConflict: provisioning state is not terminal' when a data connection started while the cluster was still transitioning.
resources.name: [concat(parameters('resourcesName'), '-WaitForAdxCluster')]

properties.scriptContent: set -e; echo "Waiting for ADX cluster ${ADX_CLUSTER_NAME} to reach a terminal provisioning state..."; az kusto cluster wait --created --name "${ADX_CLUSTER_NAME}" --resource-group "${RESOURCE_GROUP}" --subscription "${SUBSCRIPTION_ID}" --interval 15 --timeout 1500; echo "ADX cluster ${ADX_CLUSTER_NAME} provisioning state: $(az kusto cluster show --name "${ADX_CLUSTER_NAME}" --resource-group "${RESOURCE_GROUP}" --subscription "${SUBSCRIPTION_ID}" --query provisioningState -o tsv)"

resources.name: [format('{0}/{1}/{2}', variables('adxClusterName'), 'ontologies', guid(resourceId('Microsoft.Kusto/clusters/databases', split(format('{0}/{1}', variables('adxClusterName'), 'ontologies'), '/')[0], split(format('{0}/{1}', variables('adxClusterName'), 'ontologies'), '/')[1]), resourceId('Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities', concat(parameters('resourcesName'), '-Identity')), 'Admin'))]
